compositional_truth_check.P2: Systemic Loss (No visible plant matter in black pot)
compositional_truth_check.P3: Present (Black Pot with White Rabbit anchor)
New_Introductions: Electronic component (IC chip) on desk surface

vision_report.inventory_reconciliation: The registry is largely inaccurate regarding the current state of the biome. P2 and P4 are currently devoid of visible vegetative life, suggesting either dormancy or total specimen loss. P3 contains a single, struggling leaf and significant debris.

plant_audit.P1: Appears as a bare pot with a plant marker; no visible foliage.
plant_audit.P2: Empty soil surface; no signs of Mexican Mint.
plant_audit.P3: Contains one pale, chlorotic leaf and scattered eggshell fragments. The White Rabbit anchor remains.
plant_audit.P4: Not identifiable as a distinct entity; soil appears empty.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Dark, damp appearance in all pots; no visible fungal blooms, but significant lack of plant density.
biome_observations.desk_surface: Presence of a small electronic component (IC chip) near the pots, indicating non-botanical intervention.

temporal_deltas.T-5_to_T-1: Minimal change observed; the single leaf in P3 remains in a state of postural collapse. No new growth detected.
temporal_deltas.T-1_to_CURRENT: Static state. The environment appears stagnant with no recovery or further degradation of the remaining leaf.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of severe decline. P3 is the only pot with a living specimen, which exhibits signs of extreme stress (chlorosis, lack of turgor). P1, P2, and P4 are effectively non-functional as plant habitats.
anomalies: Eggshell fragments in P3 (likely a calcium supplement attempt)
Electronic component (IC chip) on desk surface
Total absence of expected P2/P4 specimens
vision_report.narrative_description: The biome is currently failing. The Pothos (P3) is the sole survivor, though it is in a precarious state. The presence of eggshells suggests an attempt at soil amendment, but the lack of growth in other pots indicates a systemic failure in the environment. The desk surface is cluttered with non-botanical debris, suggesting the area is being used for purposes other than plant cultivation.
